Subject: ICL Comment - Air Quality Mercury and Dust Particulate
nepa_comment_body__c: The draft EA fails to adequately analyze mercury contamination and dust particulate emissions from the proposed placer mining operation. Historical placer mining in Owyhee County has documented mercury releases from natural deposits. The Air Quality section does not address fugitive dust from the 1.7-mile access road or mercury particulate from active mining. The FONSI cannot be supported without this analysis.
nepa_category__c: Air Quality - Mercury Particulate; Fugitive Dust
nepa_agency_response__c: Air Quality section supplemented with dust mitigation analysis including fugitive dust from access road and mercury particulate monitoring protocol. Analysis supports FONSI determination with addition of dust suppression design feature.
nepa_litigation_threat_basis__c: Prior 9th Circuit suction dredge NEPA cases with ICL as plaintiff

Subject: OSC Comment - Sage-Grouse Lek Buffer Departure Justification
nepa_comment_body__c: The draft EA does not provide adequate justification for the apparent departure from the 3.1-mile lek buffer under the 2019 Idaho ARMPA Record of Decision. If BLM is relying on a justifiable departure provision the EA must explicitly cite the applicable ARMPA language demonstrate that impacts are within the range analyzed in the ROD and document consultation with the Idaho Governor's office.
nepa_agency_response__c: Response memo generated citing 2019 Idaho ARMPA ROD Section 4.3 Justifiable Departure. EA updated with explicit ARMPA citation and Idaho Governor's office consultation documentation.

Subject: Shoshone-Paiute Tribes - Section 106 Consultation Concerns
nepa_comment_body__c: The Shoshone-Paiute Tribes of the Duck Valley Reservation formally request that BLM document the government-to-government consultation process under NHPA Section 106 and Executive Order 13175. The 1.7-mile access road traverses land with documented traditional cultural properties in the Jordan Creek corridor. The EA must include a cultural landscape analysis and demonstrate that the Tribes have been meaningfully consulted — not merely notified — prior to any finding of no adverse effect. A Programmatic Agreement may be required.
nepa_category__c: NHPA Section 106 - Traditional Cultural Properties; Government-to-Government Consultation
nepa_agency_response__c: Government-to-government consultation with Shoshone-Paiute Tribes completed per NHPA Section 106 and E.O. 13175. Cultural landscape analysis incorporated into EA. Tribal Consultation Certification signed and added to Required Document Registry. No adverse effect determination documented.
nepa_organization__c: Shoshone-Paiute Tribes of the Duck Valley Reservation
nepa_litigation_threat_basis__c: Tribal Nation plaintiff — 87.5% litigation win rate (PermitTEC corpus); consultation failure is highest-risk grounds for NHPA Section 106 challenge
